questions.question:
Mala the Elephant collects fruit counts from many trees. Kaya the Lion tries to remember all the numbers separately.

Why does Biru the Monkey suggest using an array?
options: Arrays reduce memory usage to zero

questions.question:
Kaya the Lion stores fruit counts in an array.

Why is accessing an element by index considered fast?
options: Because the array is always small
Because the position is directly known

questions.question:
Biru the Monkey warns Kaya the Lion about indexing mistakes.

In most arrays, what is the index of the first element?

Mala the Elephant asks for the third fruit count.

Which index should Kaya the Lion use?

Kaya the Lion wants to check all elements in an array.

What does traversal mean in this context?
options: Visiting each element one by one

questions.question:
Biru the Monkey explains that inserting an element in the middle of an array is slow.

Why?

Kaya the Lion compares arrays with linked lists.

Which situation is best suited for arrays?

Kaya the Lion wants to traverse an array of size n.

One line is missing.

Which option completes the logic?
questions.code:
```
SET i = 0

WHILE i < n:
    PROCESS array[i]
    _________
```
